extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r3, whose condition is a conjunction of 12 inputs that includes immunosuppressed. rec_drug_b is set true by r2 whenever immunosuppressed is true. Because r3's firing condition entails immunosuppressed=true, r3 firing always implies r2 also fires. The two rules target different outputs (rec_drug_a vs rec_drug_b), so priority/order conflict resolution never suppresses either — they set their outputs independently. Thus any input that satisfies all 12 conjuncts of r3 forces both rec_drug_a=true (via r3) and rec_drug_b=true (via r2, since immunosuppressed is among the conjuncts), violating the mutual-exclusion property (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b). The witness sets all 12 conjuncts true (pregnancy is irrelevant, set false): r2 fires -> rec_drug_b=true, r3 fires -> rec_drug_a=true, so both are jointly true.
